harita.title: Interactive Map of Dalyan
harita.subtitle: Explore Every Corner of Dalyan
harita.heroDescription: Use our interactive map to discover all of Dalyan's attractions, landmarks, beaches, restaurants, and essential services. Filter by category to plan your perfect visit.

kaunos.name: Kaunos Ancient City
kaunos.description: One of the most important ancient cities of Caria, founded in the 10th century BCE. Features a well-preserved theater, temple ruins, Roman baths, and the city acropolis with panoramic views over the Dalyan Delta.

rockTombs.name: Lycian Rock Tombs
rockTombs.description: Magnificent temple-style tombs carved into the sheer cliff face overlooking the Dalyan River. Dating to the 4th century BCE, these tombs are among the most photographed landmarks in southwestern Turkey.

iztuzu.name: Iztuzu Beach
iztuzu.description: A pristine 4.5-kilometer sand spit separating the Dalyan River Delta from the Mediterranean Sea. This protected beach serves as a critical nesting site for endangered Caretta Caretta (loggerhead) sea turtles.

mudBaths.name: Dalyan Mud Baths
mudBaths.description: Natural mineral mud pools and adjacent thermal springs on the shore of Lake Koycegiz. Known for their skin-rejuvenating properties and legendary association with Cleopatra.

sultaniye.name: Sultaniye Hot Springs
sultaniye.description: Geothermal springs flowing at 39 degrees Celsius on the southwestern shore of Lake Koycegiz. Rich in sulfur, calcium, and magnesium minerals with documented therapeutic benefits.

dalyanRiver.name: Dalyan River & Canal
dalyanRiver.description: A 12-kilometer scenic waterway connecting Lake Koycegiz to the Mediterranean Sea. The reed-lined channels are home to diverse birdlife and provide the main route for boat tours to the area's attractions.

koycegizLake.name: Lake Koycegiz
koycegizLake.description: A large freshwater lake north of Dalyan town, surrounded by mountains and pine forests. The lake supports a rich ecosystem and offers opportunities for swimming, fishing, and kayaking.

turtleHospital.name: Sea Turtle Research Center (DEKAMER)
turtleHospital.description: A rescue and rehabilitation center for injured sea turtles located near Iztuzu Beach. Visitors can learn about conservation efforts and observe turtles being treated before their return to the wild.

boatCooperative.name: Dalyan Boat Cooperative
boatCooperative.description: The main departure point for river boat tours in the center of Dalyan town. The cooperative operates daily scheduled services to Iztuzu Beach, Kaunos, the mud baths, and Lake Koycegiz.

townCenter.name: Dalyan Town Center
townCenter.description: The charming heart of Dalyan with riverside promenades, restaurants, shops, and cafes. The main street (Maras Caddesi) runs parallel to the river and is the social hub of the town.

mosqueMosque.name: Dalyan Mosque
mosqueMosque.description: The central mosque of Dalyan town, located near the main market area. A local landmark and gathering point.

market.name: Dalyan Saturday Market
market.description: A vibrant weekly market held every Saturday morning in the town center. Local farmers sell fresh produce, olives, honey, spices, textiles, and handcrafted goods.

ekincik.name: Ekincik Bay
ekincik.description: A sheltered bay approximately 30 kilometers south of Dalyan, known for its turquoise waters and access to the Blue Cave. A popular destination for day trips by boat or road.

calbis.name: Calbis Beach
calbis.description: A quiet pebble beach along the Dalyan coast, reachable by boat. Popular with visitors seeking a more secluded alternative to Iztuzu Beach.

emptyState.title: No results found
emptyState.description: Try adjusting your filters or zooming out to see more locations on the map.
